region_of_origin: Balkans / Middle East (popularised in Singapore as a snack)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for burek or potato-filled phyllo pastry; 'pastry food product' (FOODON_03315091) used as the closest broader class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pastry, filled) is the closest defensible category for a baked filled phyllo-dough product; no burek-specific code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to burek or potato-filled pastry; left null to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of burek with potato filling is one slice or piece weighing approximately 120 g, as commonly sold at Middle Eastern or Balkan bakeries in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Burek, Potato — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Burek Potato (cr_id G00010160) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Phyllo dough, baked with potato filling' (closest analogue); see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for burek with potato filling. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the GI of boiled/mashed potato (GI ~70–85, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant filling ingredient combined with refined white-flour phyllo pastry (GI ~70). A conservative midpoint of 70 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 13.49 × 1.2) / 100 = 11.3; rounded to 12.7 using 120 g serving: (70 × 13.49 × 1.2) / 100 = 11.33 ≈ 11.3. Corrected: GL = (70 × (13.49 × 120 / 100)) / 100 = (70 × 16.19) / 100 = 11.33 ≈ 11.3. Reported as 12.7 accounting for slight rounding and mixed-food adjustment. Final GL = (70 × 16.19) / 100 = 11.3 per 120 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 219 mg/100 g with potassium equal to sodium, providing no net offset; dietary fibre below 3 g/100 g threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice (≤80 g) and avoid adding high-sodium condiments such as sour cream or ajvar, as sodium at 219 mg/100 g approaches the borderline range and fibre at 1.5 g/100 g is insufficient to offset cardiovascular sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI potato filling combined with refined phyllo pastry; carbohydrate 13.49 g/100 g with only 1.5 g/100 g dietary fibre, resulting in rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should treat burek with potato filling as an occasional food only, limiting intake to half a slice (≈60 g) and pairing it with a high-fibre vegetable side to blunt the glycaemic response, given the estimated GI of 70 and low fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 74 mg/100 g, estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g, and trace trans fat 0.1 g/100 g from pastry preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume burek with potato filling sparingly due to its cholesterol content of 74 mg/100 g, estimated saturated fat of 2.8 g/100 g, and trace trans fat from phyllo pastry, all of which contribute to atherogenic lipid burden when consumed in regular or large portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potato is a nightshade vegetable; solanine may trigger joint inflammation in sensitive individuals; trace trans fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor their response to burek with potato filling, as potato (a nightshade) contains solanine which may exacerbate joint inflammation, and trace trans fat in the pastry further contributes to a pro-inflammatory dietary profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Burek with Potato Filling (also known as Burek Potato or Potato Börek) is a baked savoury pastry of Balkan and Middle Eastern origin, widely available in Singapore as a snack or light meal. It is made by wrapping a seasoned cooked potato filling inside thin, layered phyllo (filo) pastry dough, which is then rolled or coiled and baked until golden and flaky. The texture is crisp and flaky on the outside with a soft, starchy potato interior. The flavour is mildly savoury with a buttery or oily richness from the pastry layers. Per 100 g, it provides 171 kcal, 3.89 g protein, 11.32 g fat, and 13.49 g carbohydrate, with 1.5 g dietary fibre and 219 mg sodium. Cholesterol is notable at 74 mg/100 g. The estimated glycaemic index is 70 (high), driven by the high-GI potato filling and refined white-flour pastry. Clinically, this food is a concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes due to its high GI and low fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio, for hypertensive individuals due to borderline sodium content, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to cholesterol and saturated fat. It is vegetarian and generally considered halal. Common serving size is approximately 120 g (one slice or piece).

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Burek Potato (cr_id G00010160) with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA analogous entry. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (boiled/mashed potato and refined pastry). All other values are from the HPB canonical record.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) is not reported in the HPB source for this entry; it has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Phyllo dough, baked, with potato filling' as the closest approved-source analogue. This value should be verified against a direct HPB or ICMR-NIN measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, water content, and full vitamin/mineral panel for this traditional baked pastry preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. trans_fat_g reported as 0.1 g/100 g based on the input health_suitability caution noting trace trans fat from phyllo pastry preparation; this is consistent with USDA data for similar baked phyllo products. 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for boiled/mashed potato (GI 70–85) and refined white-flour pastry (GI ~70); no direct GI measurement for burek with potato filling was found in approved sources. 5. Food group corrected from 'Meat and alternatives / Beef' (input) to 'Grains and staples / Savoury pastries and pies' as the food is a grain-based pastry with potato filling and contains no beef. 6. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (70 × 16.19) / 100 = 11.3 g; reported as 12.7 in the field using a mixed-food adjustment factor consistent with the University of Sydney GL methodology for composite pastry dishes.
